question_title: Will Rob Jetten still be listed as Prime Minister of the Netherlands on the official Dutch government cabinet page after April 30, 2026?
summary: Rob Jetten is currently the Prime Minister of the Netherlands, confirmed on the official government.nl cabinet page. He was sworn in on February 23, 2026 — only ~6 weeks ago. With 26 days until the May 1 resolution check, there is no active crisis, no-confidence motion, or coalition breakup threatening the government. Crucially, even if the government were to fall, Dutch constitutional convention means the PM remains listed as "demissionary" Prime Minister until a successor is sworn in — a process that historically takes months (Schoof remained listed ~8 months after his government collapsed). The probability of Jetten being removed from the page within 26 days is vanishingly small.

factors.description: Jetten is currently listed as PM on government.nl (confirmed via direct URL fetch of the resolution source)
Government sworn in only ~6 weeks ago (Feb 23, 2026) — no Dutch government has fallen this early in modern history
No active crisis, no-confidence motion, or coalition breakup reported as of early April 2026
Jetten actively governing (Kyiv visit, JEF summit Mar 26, Hormuz policy) — no signs of imminent collapse
Minority government (66/150 seats) inherently fragile — rated 3rd most unstable in Europe by Verisk Maplecroft
Only ~26 days until resolution — extremely short window for government collapse AND removal from page
Even if government fell, PM stays listed as 'demissionary' PM until successor sworn in — Schoof stayed ~8 months after his government collapsed
Dutch base rate: last 2 governments collapsed after 11-18 months, not within first 2 months

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.97 (97% probability YES)**

Key reasoning:
- Rob Jetten is currently listed as PM on the resolution source (government.nl)
- The government is only 6 weeks old with no active crisis
- Only 26 days remain — far too short for a government to fall AND be replaced
- The Dutch "demissionary" mechanism is the clincher: even if the coalition collapsed tomorrow, Jetten would remain listed as PM for months until a successor forms (as demonstrated by Schoof's 8-month caretaker period)
- The only NO scenario requires an unprecedented sequence of events within 26 days
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will Rob Jetten still be listed as Prime Minister of the Netherlands on the official Dutch government cabinet page after April 30, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43056)
[Rob Jetten - Simple English Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rob_Jetten)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if the Dutch government's Members of the Government page at https://www.government.nl/government/members-of-cabinet lists Rob Jetten as Prime Minister at any point on or after May 1, 2026 when checked by Metaculus. Otherwise, this question resolves to no.
